name: Leahy's Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: School District of Philadelphia (historical; cut ties 2015)
recent_news: Leahy Transportation filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in November 2015 after notifying School District of Philadelphia it had no insurance; district reassigned most of its 52 routes. A related Yelp listing as of April 2026 references ~100 buses and the Salmon St location, suggesting a successor/family operation continues, but operating status is uncertain.
fit_notes: Distressed-asset story: prior Chapter 11 and loss of Philadelphia SD contract make Leahy's a weak tuck-in candidate; only worth a look if family wants to monetize a residual private/parochial book. Verify operating status before any outreach.

name: Powder River Transportation
owner_or_parent: Wynne Transportation (acquired from Coach USA bankruptcy Aug 2024; Wynne itself filed Chapter 11 in Jan 2025)
districts_or_customers: Gillette/Campbell County area charter and group transportation; coal-mine commuter shuttles historically
recent_news: Powder River was a Coach USA subsidiary; in Aug 2024 the U.S. Bankruptcy Court approved its sale to Wynne Transportation as part of Coach USA's Chapter 11 363 sale. Wynne Transportation Holdings itself filed Chapter 11 in Jan 2025 after a $32.8M judgment, putting the asset's status in flux.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in target: Powder River is a former Coach USA charter/transit unit now under PE-backed Wynne Transportation (itself in Chapter 11), and is primarily motorcoach/transit rather than yellow school-bus contracted routes. Skip unless Summit specifically wants Western charter capacity.

name: Bowen's Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: School District of Rhinelander (sole contractor since 2009, ~2,000 students/day, 39 routes)
recent_news: Founded 1957 by Andy and Ann Bowen; incorporated 1974; current owners Jeff and Wendy Bowen acquired in 1996. Became sole contractor for Rhinelander School District in 2009, expanding from 12 to 39 routes; added charter coach service plus ancillary excavating, snowplowing, and vehicle service lines.
fit_notes: Attractive Northern Wisconsin tuck-in: sole-contractor relationship with Rhinelander SD provides route stability and pricing power; second-generation owners (Jeff/Wendy since 1996) approaching typical succession window. Non-core excavating/snowplow assets could be carved out at close.

name: RW Fulkerson Bus & Bus Parts
districts_or_customers: Possibly Cole Camp R-1 and nearby Benton County, MO districts (unverified)
recent_news: No web footprint found in dedicated searches; no website, no recent news, no obituaries or sale announcements identified. Name suggests a hybrid bus-parts dealer plus small-fleet operator, which is often a dual-use/repair-shop family business rather than a pure school-bus contractor.
fit_notes: Low confidence target: business may be primarily a bus-parts/repair operation rather than a school-bus contractor. Recommend phone-verification before further work; if it is a real route operator in Benton County, the small scale and dealer/parts mix make it a marginal tuck-in at best.

name: Tucker Transportation
districts_or_customers: Anaconda Public Schools (K-12 and Special Needs)
Charter/tour customers across Butte, Helena, Great Falls and Montana City
recent_news: Family-owned charter motorcoach and Montana school-bus contractor founded by 'Tucker' McGree and son-in-law Bernie Brophy ~1995; Dan McGree continues to run the business from a three-acre Butte HQ at 503 Centennial Ave with additional facilities in Anaconda and Montana City. Operates Prevost motorcoaches alongside daily K-12 and special-needs school routes in Anaconda.
fit_notes: Solid small Montana tuck-in: established second-generation family operator with contracted Anaconda K-12/SPED routes plus high-end Prevost charter book; founder-era principals likely facing succession. Charter exposure is a positive ancillary but adds operational complexity vs pure yellow-bus model.
